# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

---

## PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### **Question 1** `[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]`

**Q: Differentiate between leadership behaviour and leadership attitude with suitable examples.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

| Aspect | Leadership Behaviour | Leadership Attitude |
|---|---|---|
| **Definition** | The observable actions a leader performs | The internal beliefs and values a leader holds |
| **Visibility** | External, measurable, observable | Internal, psychological, often implicit |
| **Example** | A manager conducting a daily stand-up meeting | A manager believing that "people are self-motivated" |
| **Formation** | Shaped by training, experience, modelling | Shaped by upbringing, culture, education |

> **Valuation Tip:** Award 1.5 marks for each clear distinction; 0.5 mark for example. Total 3 marks.

---

### **Question 2** `[KTU University Exam – May 2024]`

**Q: Briefly explain Goleman's concept of Emotional Intelligence and its relevance to effective leadership.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

Daniel Goleman defined **Emotional Intelligence (EI)** as the ability to perceive, control, evaluate, and express one's own emotions, as well as to handle interpersonal relationships judiciously and empathetically. It consists of **five components**: Self-Awareness, Self-Regulation, Motivation, Empathy, and Social Skill.

**Relevance to Leadership:** Effective leaders must regulate their emotions, empathize with followers, motivate teams intrinsically, and manage social dynamics — all EI competencies. Goleman found EI contributes **67%** to leadership effectiveness at senior levels.

> **Valuation Tip:** Award 1 mark for definition + 5 components, 1 mark for leadership relevance, 1 mark for the 67% statistic and EI–performance linkage. Total 3 marks.

---

## PART B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks, Internal Choice)

---

### **Question A (14 Marks)** `[KTU University Exam – May 2024]`

**Q: "Effective leadership emerges from the alignment of appropriate behaviours with constructive attitudes." Critically analyze this statement with reference to (a) Blake and Mouton's Managerial Grid and (b) McGregor's Theory X and Theory Y.**

---

#### **Part (a) — Blake and Mouton's Managerial Grid (7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Introduction (1 mark):** The Blake-Mouton Managerial Grid, developed in 1964, is a two-dimensional framework that plots **Concern for Production (X-axis)** against **Concern for People (Y-axis)** on a **1–9 scale** each.

**Five Major Styles (4 marks):**

1. **(1,1) Impoverished Management** — Minimal concern for both; disengaged, "survive the day" approach.
2. **(1,9) Country Club Management** — High people concern, low production; friendly but unproductive.
3. **(9,1) Authority-Compliance (Produce-or-Perish)** — High production, low people; efficient but exploitative.
4. **(5,5) Middle-of-the-Road Management** — Moderate concern on both; balanced compromise, mediocre outcomes.
5. **(9,9) Team Management** — High concern on both; synergistic, highest sustainable effectiveness.

**Analysis (2 marks):** Blake and Mouton argued that the **(9,9)** style is universally most effective because it demonstrates that **production and people are complementary**, not opposing. The grid reveals that leaders can be high on both dimensions simultaneously — by empowering competent people, production naturally rises.

> **Valuation Tip:** Award 1 mark for grid description, 4 marks for the five styles, 2 marks for analysis. Missing any of the five styles costs 0.5–1 mark each.

---

#### **Part (b) — McGregor's Theory X and Theory Y (7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Introduction (1 mark):** Douglas McGregor's 1960 theory proposed that managerial behaviour is a direct consequence of managerial **attitudes** about human nature. Two contrasting assumption systems determine leadership effectiveness.

**Theory X Assumptions (2 marks):**
- Workers inherently dislike work.
- Workers must be coerced, controlled, directed, and threatened with punishment.
- Workers avoid responsibility and prefer direction.

**Theory Y Assumptions (2 marks):**
- Work is as natural as play or rest when conditions are favourable.
- Workers are capable of self-direction and seek responsibility.
- Workers can exercise creativity in problem-solving.

**Critical Analysis (2 marks):** McGregor's critical insight is that managerial **attitude determines managerial behaviour**. A Theory X manager will implement authoritarian, control-based leadership (matching the (9,1) grid style), producing compliant but disengaged subordinates. A Theory Y manager will implement participative, empowering leadership (matching the (9,9) style), producing motivated, innovative subordinates. The theory demonstrates that the **most effective leadership attitude** is one that trusts human capability — Theory Y — which then translates into the most effective leadership behaviour.

> **Valuation Tip:** Award 1 mark for introduction, 2 marks for Theory X, 2 marks for Theory Y, 2 marks for attitude–behaviour linkage analysis.

---

### **Question B (14 Marks) — Alternative Choice** `[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]`

**Q: Discuss the determinants of effective leadership by analyzing (a) the Ohio State Studies framework and (b) Hersey and Blanchard's Situational Leadership Model with reference to follower readiness.**

---

#### **Part (a) — Ohio State Studies (7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Background (1 mark):** Conducted in the 1940s–50s by researchers Stogdill and Coons at Ohio State University to identify independent dimensions of leader behaviour.

**Two Independent Dimensions (3 marks):**

1. **Initiating Structure (Task Behaviour):** The extent to which a leader defines and organizes work, clarifies roles, schedules activities, and assigns tasks. This is the **task-oriented** dimension.

2. **Consideration (People Behaviour):** The extent to which a leader shows concern, respect, trust, and warmth toward subordinates. This is the **relationship-oriented** dimension.

**Critical Insight (2 marks):** Unlike earlier one-dimensional theories, Ohio State studies proved that these two dimensions are **independent** — a leader can score high or low on both. Research found that leaders scoring high on **both** dimensions (high structure + high consideration) typically had subordinates with higher performance and satisfaction.

**Leadership Quadrant (1 mark):**

| | High Consideration | Low Consideration |
|---|---|---|
| **High Structure** | Most effective leadership | Authoritarian, demanding |
| **Low Structure** | Permissive, friendly | Disengaged, indifferent |

> **Valuation Tip:** Award 1 mark for background, 3 marks for two dimensions, 2 marks for independence insight, 1 mark for quadrant.

---

#### **Part (b) — Hersey and Blanchard's Situational Leadership Model (7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Introduction (1 mark):** Developed by Paul Hersey and Ken Blanchard in 1969 (revised 1977), this model asserts that **the most effective leadership style depends on the readiness level of followers**.

**Four Follower Readiness Levels (3 marks):**

1. **R1 — Unable and Unwilling (Incompetent and Insecure):** Followers lack capability and confidence.
2. **R2 — Unable but Willing (Incompetent but Confident):** Followers lack skill but are motivated.
3. **R3 — Able but Unwilling (Competent but Unwilling):** Followers have skills but lack motivation or confidence.
4. **R4 — Able and Willing (Competent and Confident):** Followers are both skilled and motivated.

**Four Leadership Styles Matched to Readiness (2 marks):**

| Readiness | Style | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | **Telling (S1)** | High Task, Low Relationship — give clear instructions |
| R2 | **Selling (S2)** | High Task, High Relationship — coach and persuade |
| R3 | **Participating (S3)** | Low Task, High Relationship — share decisions |
| R4 | **Delegating (S4)** | Low Task, Low Relationship — empower autonomy |

**Analysis (1 mark):** Effective leaders diagnose follower readiness and **adapt their behaviour** accordingly. The same leader may use S1 with a new trainee and S4 with an experienced specialist.

> **Valuation Tip:** Award 1 mark for introduction, 3 marks for R1–R4, 2 marks for S1–S4 matching, 1 mark for analysis.

---

## ⚠️ KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Marks-Loss Pitfalls in Leadership Behaviour & Attitude Questions:**
>
> 1. **Confusing Behaviour with Attitude** — Writing "Theory X is a behaviour" or "Authoritarian style is an attitude." Correct: Theory X is an **attitude**; the *manifestation* of Theory X in action is behaviour.
> 2. **Listing without comparing** — When asked to differentiate, students often only describe one concept. Always **explicitly compare** using parallel structure.
> 3. **Missing the two-dimensional nature** — In Ohio State Studies, students often treat Initiating Structure and Consideration as opposites. They are **independent axes**, not opposite poles.
> 4. **Forgetting the 67% EI statistic** — Goleman's research percentage is a high-yield factoid; omitting it costs 0.5–1 mark.
> 5. **Not linking back to effectiveness** — When discussing styles, always conclude with **which is most effective and why** — KTU examiners award marks for evaluative judgment, not mere description.
> 6. **Missing internal-choice logic** — In 14-mark questions, ensure **both sub-parts (a) and (b)** receive roughly equal treatment (~7 marks each). Skewing content allocation loses 2–3 marks.

---

## 📋 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

### ✅ Rapid Revision Checklist — Module 1: Effective Leadership Behaviours and Attitudes

- [ ] **Behaviour = observable action; Attitude = internal belief.** They are interdependent but distinct.
- [ ] **Ohio State Studies:** Two independent dimensions — **Initiating Structure** (task) and **Consideration** (people).
- [ ] **Michigan Studies:** Job-centred vs. Employee-centred leaders; employee-centred linked to higher productivity and satisfaction.
- [ ] **Blake-Mouton Grid:** Two axes, scale 1–9; five styles — (1,1), (1,9), (9,1), (5,5), **(9,9)** most effective.
- [ ] **McGregor Theory X:** Authoritarian attitude — workers dislike work, avoid responsibility, need control.
- [ ] **McGregor Theory Y:** Democratic attitude — work is natural, people seek responsibility, are creative.
- [ ] **Goleman EI:** Five components — Self-Awareness, Self-Regulation, Motivation, Empathy, Social Skill. **EI contributes 67%** to senior leadership effectiveness.
- [ ] **Fiedler:** Effectiveness = match between LPC-based leader style and situational favourableness (LMR + TS + PP).
- [ ] **Hersey-Blanchard:** Four readiness levels (R1–R4) matched to four styles (Telling, Selling, Participating, Delegating).
- [ ] **Path-Goal (House):** Directive, Supportive, Participative, Achievement-Oriented — leaders clarify paths to goals.
- [ ] **Transformational vs. Transactional:** Vision-driven inspiration vs. exchange-based compliance.
- [ ] **Critical formula to memorize:** $\text{Managerial Behaviour} = f(\text{Managerial Attitude})$
- [ ] **Most effective leadership style per KTU expectations:** **(9,9) Team Management** aligned with **Theory Y attitude** and **high Emotional Intelligence**.

---

<!-- SECTION_5_END -->
